title: portulaca oleracea var. sylvestris
intro: Portulaca oleracea var. sylvestris, also known as the common purslane or wild purslane, is a succulent plant with stems that are rich in mucilages and edible leaves that contain high levels of omega-3 fatty acids. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Portulaca oleracea var. sylvestris include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and lowering blood pressure, as well as being used as a natural treatment for skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea by steeping the leaves in hot water, or used in salads, and is also dried and made into capsules or tinctures for its medicinal properties.
